phase_history.notes: First APC pipeline test. Concept locked: static gain/trim utility with Input Gain, Output Trim, and a peak meter. Clean modern dark UI.

DSP graph: input gain -> meter tap -> output trim, host-handled bypass. Single-pass implementation. WebView chosen over Visage per README's 'Visage in active testing' caveat; first-target DAW is Reaper.

v1 design adapted from amber-signal: 520x260 horizontal strip — Input knob | L/R peak meter | Output knob, amber accents on charcoal. v1-test.html previewed and approved by transitioning straight into /impl.
VST3 built successfully in Release x64. WebView2 SDK 1.0.3485.44 installed via NuGet at CurrentUser scope as a one-time prerequisite. Both WebView validators passed before build.

Verified live in FL Studio: UI renders, both knobs update parameters in real time, meter pumps via 30 Hz timer + window.updateMeters(), bypass toggle works. Final clean build at C:/Program Files/Common Files/VST3/SimpleGain.vst3 (5,630,976 B). Root cause of the initial silent-controls bug: my resource provider was stripping 'https://juce.backend/' (with trailing slash) but JUCE's Windows native (juce_WebBrowserComponent_windows.cpp:884) actually strips 'https://juce.backend' (no trailing slash) before invoking the callback — so every request returned index.html for everything. Fix: strip the leading slash directly instead of trying to subtract the root.

framework_selection.rationale: Level-1 complexity, but WebView is the more battle-tested UI path in this repo (README flags Visage as 'in active testing, may be unstable on some hosts'). WebView gives faster design-phase iteration (live HTML reload) and a dedicated skill_design_webview quick-start. UI overhead is negligible at this complexity; the audio thread is untouched by the choice.
